channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: Childcare costs rise 9 per cent as inflation pressures continue to challenge providers
item.description: Childcare costs increased by 9.0 per cent in the year to April 2026, according to the latest Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) data, placing continued pressure on early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers as operating expenses outpace broader inflation.
item.content:encoded: Childcare costs increased by 9.0 per cent in the year to April 2026, according to the latest Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) data, placing continued pressure on early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers as operating expenses outpace broader inflation.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Child wellbeing and prevention key to youth justice reform, ARACY report finds
item.description: Australia's approach to youth justice should focus more heavily on child wellbeing, prevention and early intervention rather than detention, according to a new report from the Australian Research Alliance for Children and Youth (ARACY).
item.content:encoded: Australia's approach to youth justice should focus more heavily on child wellbeing, prevention and early intervention rather than detention, according to a new report from the Australian Research Alliance for Children and Youth (ARACY).
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Affordable preschool at risk after community preschools hit with 15 per cent unfunded wages increase in 6 months
item.description: NSW community preschools are struggling to keep fees affordable after facing a 15 per cent increase in wage costs in just six months.
item.content:encoded: NSW community preschools are struggling to keep fees affordable after facing a 15 per cent increase in wage costs in just six months.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Same framework, different conditions: why quality in OSHC needs its own language
item.description: When Australia talks about quality in early childhood education and care, the conversation often centres on long day care and preschool/kindergarten. These settings are critical, of course. But they are not the whole story.
item.content:encoded: When Australia talks about quality in early childhood education and care, the conversation often centres on long day care and preschool/kindergarten. These settings are critical, of course. But they are not the whole story.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: New library partnership helping Port Hedland children build a love of reading
item.description: The Y WA’s Port Hedland Early Learning Centre (ELC) has partnered with Town of Port Hedland Libraries through a new early literacy initiative aimed at giving local children the strongest possible start in life.
item.content:encoded: The Y WA’s Port Hedland Early Learning Centre (ELC) has partnered with Town of Port Hedland Libraries through a new early literacy initiative aimed at giving local children the strongest possible start in life.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: G8 Education elevates people and culture leader to COO role amid business transformation
item.description: G8 Education has appointed current Chief People and Culture Officer Linda Carroll as Chief Operating Officer, signalling the growing importance of workforce, culture and safety leadership as the provider continues a business-wide transformation program.
item.content:encoded: G8 Education has appointed current Chief People and Culture Officer Linda Carroll as Chief Operating Officer, signalling the growing importance of workforce, culture and safety leadership as the provider continues a business-wide transformation program.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples ready to work in partnership for a safer future for Queensland children
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 5 June 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC warns proposed NT child protection laws will make children less safe
item.description: SNAICC has warned that the Northern Territory Government's proposed NT child protection laws will make children less safe.

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 29 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to NT child protection reforms, National Sorry Day, AbSec’s call for systemic change, the launch of the Bushland Animal Yoga Cards, and continued Budget coverage.

item.title: From Country to Classroom: SNAICC Launches Culturally Grounded Learning Tools
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.content:encoded: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: SNAICC Raises Alarm Over Narrow NT Child Protection Inquiry Scope
item.description: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC says narrow scope of NT Child Protection Inquiry fail to address systemic issues needed to keep Aboriginal children and families safe.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 15 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, the Federal Budget was a missed opportunity for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. NT Government has introduced legislation that undermine the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle. And a heartwarming Mother's Day story from a member of SNAICC's Youth Voice advisory group on how love, kinship and culture can help children in out-of-home-care grow up Deadly.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC
